name: Telegramtrigger Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Telegramtrigger Workflow. This workflow integrates 8 different services: telegramTrigger, httpRequest, stickyNote, telegram, merge. It contains 27 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
SQL query to create table in Supabase:

```
create table
  public.telegram_users (
    id uuid not null default gen_random_uuid (),
    date_created timestamp with time zone not null default (now() at time zone 'utc'::text),
    telegram_id bigint null,
    openai_thread_id text null,
    constraint telegram_users_pkey primary key (id)
  ) tablespace pg_default;
```

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note5

parameters.content:
### Set up steps
1. **Create a Telegram Bot** using the [Botfather]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} and obtain the bot token.
2. **Set up Supabase:**
	1. Create a new project and generate a ```SUPABASE_URL``` and ```SUPABASE_KEY```.
	2. Create a new table named ```telegram_users``` with the following SQL query:
```
create table
  public.telegram_users (
    id uuid not null default gen_random_uuid (),
    date_created timestamp with time zone not null default (now() at time zone 'utc'::text),
    telegram_id bigint null,
    openai_thread_id text null,
    constraint telegram_users_pkey primary key (id)
  ) tablespace pg_default;
```
3. **OpenAI Setup:**
	1. Create an OpenAI assistant and obtain the ```OPENAI_API_KEY```.
	2. Customize your assistant’s personality or use cases according to your requirements.
4. **Environment Configuration in n8n:**
	1. Configure the Telegram, Supabase, and OpenAI nodes with the appropriate credentials.
	2. Set up triggers for receiving messages and handling conversation logic.
	3. Set up OpenAI assistant ID in "++OPENAI - Run assistant++" node.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.content:
SQL query to create table in Supabase:

```
create table
  public.telegram_users (
    id uuid not null default gen_random_uuid (),
    date_created timestamp with time zone not null default (now() at time zone 'utc'::text),
    telegram_id bigint null,
```

Create assistant in [OpenAI]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

**Specify own assistant id here**
Create own Telegram bot in [Botfather bot]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
Create table in [Supabase]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} with SQL query

### Set up steps
1. **Create a Telegram Bot** using the [Botfather]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} and obtain the bot token.
2. **Set up Supabase:**
	1. Create a new project and generate a ```SUPABASE_URL``` and ```SUPABASE_KEY```.
	2. Create a new table named ```telegram_users``` with the following SQL query:
```
create table
  public.telegram_users (
    id uuid not null default gen_random_uuid (),
    date_created timestamp with time zone not null default (now() at time zone 'utc'::text),
    telegram_id bigint null,
    openai_thread_id text null,
    constraint telegram_users_pkey primary key (id)
  ) tablespace pg_default;
```
3. **OpenAI Setup:**
	1. Create an OpenAI assistant and obtain the ```OPENAI_API_KEY```.
	2. Customize your assistant’s personality or use cases according to your requirements.
4. **Environment Configuration in n8n:**
	1. Configure the Telegram, Supabase, and OpenAI nodes with the appropriate credentials.
	2. Set up triggers for receiving messages and handling conversation logic.
	3. Set up OpenAI assistant ID in "++OPENAI - Run assistant++" node.

### ... or watch set up video [5 min]
[![Youtube Thumbnail]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Get New Message".
Open the "OPENAI - Create thread" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create User" node (n8n-nodes-base.supabase) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Merge" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OPENAI - Send message" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OPENAI - Run assistant" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OPENAI - Get messages" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Message to User" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If User exists" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Find User" node (n8n-nodes-base.supabase) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
